title: Out of Distribution Detection via Neural Network Anchoring
problem: Out of Distribution (OOD) detection is challenging, requiring an effective characterization of the In Distribution (ID) data manifold to distinguish between ID and OOD samples.
limitations_of_existing_approaches: Simple prediction confidence scores (like MSP) are poorly calibrated
Many state-of-the-art formulations require additional unlabeled data for calibration
Calibration strategies often rely on temperature scaling with a scalar value for all samples, not sample-specific

proposed_method.name: Neural Network Anchoring
proposed_method.description: A novel training procedure that transforms input images into tuples with anchors, predicts labels based on these tuples, and uses heteroscedastic temperature scaling for improved OOD detection.
components: Transform input image x into a tuple [c, x - c] where c is a randomly chosen image from the training set
Predict the label for x using this tuple
Consistency training strategy by perturbing the anchor before encoding
During inference, obtain predictions from multiple random anchors and estimate temperature based on standard deviation of these predictions

theoretical_foundation.finding: The heteroscedastic temperature estimate is closely related to the epistemic uncertainty of the model

results: The method can improve existing OOD detectors via heteroscedastic temperature scaling.
